name: NAFv4-ADMBw
notes: The NATO Architecture Framework (NAF) is an Enterprise Architecture framework developed by the NATO and contributing nations. This MDG technologie ist based on version 4.0 of the framework. The architecture data model of the Bundeswehr (ADMBw) is the extension of the NAF according to the requirements of the Bundeswehr.

nodeTypes.label: A1 - Meta-Data Definitions
nodeTypes.description: Architectures, particularly large ones, usually require meta-data tags to aid with searching and discovery.

nodeTypes.label: A2 - Architecture Products
nodeTypes.description: The A2 Viewpoint specifies the structure of an architecture, and the products that describe the architecture. Each product may correspond to an architecture view. This viewpoint also traces the architectures onto the Enterprise Phases they correspond to (see also C2 � Enterprise Vision) and identifies the key stakeholders, their concerns and the products that address those concerns (from ISO/IEC/IEEE Standards).

nodeTypes.label: A3 - Architecture Correspondence
nodeTypes.description: ISO/IEC/IEEE42010 introduces the idea of architecture correspondence and correspondence rules. Quoting from ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010; �A correspondence defines a relation between AD (Architecture Description) elements. Correspondences are used to express architecture relations of interest within an architecture description (or between architecture descriptions). Correspondences can be governed by correspondence rules. Correspondence rules are used to enforce relations within an architecture description (or between architecture descriptions).�

nodeTypes.label: A4 - Methodology Used
nodeTypes.description: The A4 Viewpoint specifies the methodology used for the architecting activities. This methodology is described in NAFv4 Chapter 2. The content of the A4 viewpoint can also be modeled in the A1 viewpoint.

nodeTypes.label: A5 - Architecture Status
nodeTypes.description: Since NAFv3.0, it has been possible to assign version numbers to views and specify their approval dates. In NAFv4.0, this is integrated with the meta-model�s generic project and milestone approach so that architecture releases are tracked against the architecture delivery project. The content of the A5 viewpoint can also be modeled in the A1 viewpoint.

nodeTypes.label: A6 - Architecture Version
nodeTypes.description: Where the A5 Viewpoint shows the current version number and approval status for the architecture, the A6 Viewpoint shows the complete history of the architecture.

nodeTypes.label: A7 - Architecture Compliance
nodeTypes.description: The A7 Viewpoint gives the overall specification of architecture meta-data.

nodeTypes.label: A8 - Standards
nodeTypes.description: The A8 Viewpoint encompasses both technical and non-technical standards. The standards specified in the A8 view can be applied across the architecture to a variety of structural and behavioural elements. Standards are essential to the coherent running of businesses and to the delivery of reliable, interoperable systems.

nodeTypes.label: Achiever
nodeTypes.description: An ActualResource, ActualProject or ActualEnterprisePhase that can deliver a DesiredEffect.

nodeTypes.label: ActualCondition
nodeTypes.description: An individual describing an actual situation with respect to circumstances under which an OperationalActivity, Function or ServiceFunction can be performed.

nodeTypes.label: ActualEnduringTask
nodeTypes.description: An actual undertaking recognized by an enterprise as being essential to achieving its goals - i.e. a strategic specification of what the enterprise does.

nodeTypes.label: ActualEnterprisePhase
nodeTypes.description: The ActualState that describes the phase of an Enterprise endeavor.

nodeTypes.label: ActualEnvironment
nodeTypes.description: The ActualState that describes the circumstances of an Environment.

nodeTypes.label: ActualLocation
nodeTypes.description: The ActualState that describes a physical location, for example using text to provide an address, Geo-coordinates, etc.

nodeTypes.label: ActualMeasurement
nodeTypes.description: An actual value that is applied to a Measurement.

nodeTypes.label: ActualMeasurementSet
nodeTypes.description: A set of ActualMeasurements.

nodeTypes.label: ActualOrganization
nodeTypes.description: An actual formal or informal organizational unit, e.g. "Driving and Vehicle Licensing Agency", "UAF team Alpha".

nodeTypes.label: ActualOrganizationalResource
nodeTypes.description: Abstract element for an ActualOrganization, ActualPerson or ActualPost.

nodeTypes.label: ActualOrganizationRole
nodeTypes.description: An ActualOrganizationalResource that is applied to a ResourceRole.

nodeTypes.label: ActualPerson
nodeTypes.description: An individual human being.

nodeTypes.label: ActualPost
nodeTypes.description: An actual, specific post, an instance of a Post "type" - e.g., "President of the United States of America." where the Post would be president.

nodeTypes.label: ActualProject
nodeTypes.description: A time-limited endeavor to provide a specific set of ActualResource that meet specific Capability needs.

nodeTypes.label: ActualProjectMilestone
nodeTypes.description: An event with a start date in a ActualProject from which progress is measured.

nodeTypes.label: ActualProjectMilestoneRole
nodeTypes.description: An ActualProjectMilestone that is applied to a ProjectMilestoneRole.

nodeTypes.label: ActualPropertySet
nodeTypes.description: A set or collection of Actual properties.

nodeTypes.label: ActualResource
nodeTypes.description: Role in an Organisation, where the role carries the authority to undertake a function - through the ActualOrganizationalResource given the role has the responsibility.

nodeTypes.label: ActualResourceRole
nodeTypes.description: An instance of a ResourcePerformer.

nodeTypes.label: ActualResponsibleResource
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type grouping responsible OrganizationalResources.

nodeTypes.label: ActualService
nodeTypes.description: An individual ServiceSpecification.

nodeTypes.label: ActualServiceSpecificationRole
nodeTypes.description: An instance of a ServiceSpecification in context of a ServiceSpecification.

nodeTypes.label: ActualState
nodeTypes.description: Abstract element that applies temporal extent to a set of elements realized as Instance Specifications.

nodeTypes.label: Alias
nodeTypes.description: A metamodel Artifact used to define an alternative name for an element.

nodeTypes.label: Ar - Architecture Roadmap
nodeTypes.description: The Ar Viewpoint shows the history of the architecture project as well as its future direction.

nodeTypes.label: ArchitecturalDescription
nodeTypes.description: An Architecture Description is a work product used to express the Architecture of some System Of Interest. It provides executive-level summary information about the architecture description in a consistent form to allow quick reference and comparison between architecture descriptions -- It includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program.

nodeTypes.label: Architecture
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type that represents a generic architecture. Subtypes are OperationalArchitecture and ResourceArchitecture.

nodeTypes.label: ArchitectureMetadata
nodeTypes.description: Information associated with an ArchitecturalDescription, that supplements the standard set of tags used to summarize the Architecture. It states things like what methodology was used, notation, etc.

nodeTypes.label: Asset
nodeTypes.description: Asset as applied to Security views, an abstract type that indicates the types of elements that can be considered as a subject for security analysis.

nodeTypes.label: AssetRole
nodeTypes.description: AssetRole as applied to Security views, an abstract element that indicates the type of elements  that can be considered as a subject for security analysis in the particular context (currently no security viewpoints in the framework).

nodeTypes.label: BusinessProcess
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type that represents a behavior or process (i.e. a Function or OperationalActivity) that can be performed by a Performer.

nodeTypes.label: BWRequirement
nodeTypes.description: Abstract base class for requirements.

attributes.name: Uuid
attributes.description: Maschinenlesbare Nummerierung f�r das Requirements Management Tool

nodeTypes.label: C1 - Capability Taxonomy
nodeTypes.description:
The C1 Viewpoint specifies all the capabilities that are referenced throughout one or more architectures � i.e. one C1 may provide the definitive list of capabilities for a number of logical and resource architectures.
The capabilities may be organised into specialisation hierarchies (taxonomies). Measures of Effectiveness (MoE) may be specified for each capability. Note that MoEs are inherited down a capability taxonomy.

nodeTypes.label: C1-S1 - Capability to Service Mapping
nodeTypes.description: A C1-S1 Viewpoint presents a simple mapping of services to capabilities, showing which services contribute to which capability.

nodeTypes.label: C2 - Enterprise Vision
nodeTypes.description: The purpose of the C2 Viewpoint is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the architecture and to specify the scope for the architecture. The C2 Viewpoint is high-level and describes the vision, goals, enduring tasks and capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers.

nodeTypes.label: C3 - Capability Dependencies
nodeTypes.description: The C3 Viewpoint is intended to provide a means of analysing the dependencies between capabilities and between capability clusters. The composition of capabilities (into clusters) is logical and the purpose of the clusters is to guide enterprise management.

nodeTypes.label: C4 - Standard Processes
nodeTypes.description: The C4 Viewpoint specifies Standard Operational Activities that can be re-used across multiple logical architectures (e.g. in L4, Logical Activities).

nodeTypes.label: C5 - Effects
nodeTypes.description: The purpose of the C5 Viewpoint is to describe the operational effect of the capabilities, activities and services according to the expected goals. The C5 Viewpoint is high-level description that should be easily understood by non-technical readers.

nodeTypes.label: C7 - Performance Parameters
nodeTypes.description: In the C7 Viewpoint the capability requirements (and existing capabilities) can be expressed in terms of Measures of Effectiveness (MoEs). These are high-level metrics used to judge the level of capability.

nodeTypes.label: C8 - Planning Assumptions
nodeTypes.description: The C8 Viewpoint is concerned with identification and description of assumptions that have been made for the implementation of capabilities. Assumptions can be expressed by means of requirements.

nodeTypes.label: Capability
nodeTypes.description: A high level specification of the enterprise's ability to execute a specified course of action.

nodeTypes.label: CapabilityConfiguration
nodeTypes.description: A composite structure representing the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise, assembled to meet a capability).

attributes.id: Fulfilled C5 requirements catalogue of the BSI
attributes.name: Fulfilled C5 requirements catalogue of the BSI

nodeTypes.label: CapabilityRole
nodeTypes.description: A high level specification of the enterprise's ability to execute a specified course of action.

nodeTypes.label: CapableElement
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type that represents a structural element that can perform behaviors (i.e. OperationalActivity).

nodeTypes.label: Classification
nodeTypes.description: Classification according to STANAG 1059.

nodeTypes.label: Competence
nodeTypes.description: A specific set of abilities defined by knowledge, skills and aptitude.

nodeTypes.label: ConceptItem
nodeTypes.description: Abstract, an item which may feature in a HighLevelOperationalConcept.

nodeTypes.label: ConceptRole
nodeTypes.description: Usage of a ConceptItem in the context of a HighLevelOperationalConcept.

nodeTypes.label: Concern
nodeTypes.description: Interest in an EnterprisePhase (EnterprisePhase is synonym for System in ISO 42010) relevant to one or more of its stakeholders.

nodeTypes.label: Condition
nodeTypes.description: A type that defines the Location, Environment and/or GeoPoliticalExtent.

nodeTypes.label: Cr - Capability Roadmap
nodeTypes.description: The Cr Viewpont supports the Capability Audit process by providing a method to identify gaps or duplications in capability provision. Cr indicates capability increments, which are derived from delivery milestones within acquisition projects.

nodeTypes.label: DataElement
nodeTypes.description: A formalized representation of data that is managed by or exchanged between resources.

nodeTypes.label: DataModel
nodeTypes.description: A structural specification of data types, showing relationships between them that is devoid of implementation detail. The type of data captured in the DataModel is described using the enumaration DataModelKind (Conceptual,Logical and Physical).

nodeTypes.label: DataRole
nodeTypes.description: A usage of DataElement that exists in the context of an ResourceAsset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of DataElements.

nodeTypes.label: Definition
nodeTypes.description: A comment containing a description of an element in the architecture.

nodeTypes.label: Desirer
nodeTypes.description: Abstract type used to group architecture elements that might desire a particular effect.

nodeTypes.label: DocumentReference
nodeTypes.description: The element describes a regulation, instruction or a general document.

nodeTypes.label: EnduringTask
nodeTypes.description: A type of template behavior recognized by an enterprise as being essential to achieving its goals - i.e. a template for a strategic specification of what the enterprise does.

nodeTypes.label: Energy
nodeTypes.description: A representation of any kind of energy.

nodeTypes.label: EnterpriseGoal
nodeTypes.description: A statement about a state or condition of the enterprise to be brought about or sustained through appropriate Means. An EnterpriseGoal amplifies an EnterpriseVision that is, it indicates what must be satisfied on a continuing basis to effectively attain the EnterpriseVision.

nodeTypes.label: EnterprisePhase
nodeTypes.description: A current or future state of the wholeLifeEnterprise or another EnterprisePhase.

nodeTypes.label: EnterpriseVision
nodeTypes.description: A Vision describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved.

nodeTypes.label: Environment
nodeTypes.description: A definition of the environmental factors in which something exists or functions. The definition of an Environment element can be further defined using EnvironmentKind.

nodeTypes.label: EnvironmentProperty
nodeTypes.description: A property of an Environment that is typed by a Condition.  The kinds of Condition that can be represented are Location, GeoPoliticalExtentType and Environment.

nodeTypes.label: Exchange
nodeTypes.description: Abstract tuple, grouping OperationalExchanges and ResourceExchanges that exchange Resources.

nodeTypes.label: ExchangeItem
nodeTypes.description: An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Assets and conveyed by an Exchange.

nodeTypes.label: FieldedCapability
nodeTypes.description: An individual, fully-realized capability.

nodeTypes.label: Finding
nodeTypes.description: An ascertainment made in the model, which relates to the methodology used, the subject under consideration, the tool or something else.

nodeTypes.label: FitCriterion
nodeTypes.description: This element represents an acceptance criterion for a functional or non-functional requirement.

nodeTypes.label: FulfilmentCriterion
nodeTypes.description: This element represents a criterion for evaluating the degree of implementation of a functional or non-functional requirement.

nodeTypes.label: Function
nodeTypes.description: An Activity which is specified in the context to the ResourcePerformer (human or machine) that IsCapableToPerform it.

nodeTypes.label: FunctionAction
nodeTypes.description: A call of a Function indicating that the Function is performed by a ResourceRole in a specific context.

nodeTypes.label: FunctionalRequirement
nodeTypes.description: The element represents a functional requirement (what should the system / software be able to do?).

nodeTypes.label: FunctionEdge
nodeTypes.description: A tuple that shows the flow of Resources (objects/data) between FunctionActions.

nodeTypes.label: GeoPoliticalExtentType
nodeTypes.description: A geospatial extent whose boundaries are defined by declaration or agreement by political parties.

nodeTypes.label: HighLevelOperationalConcept
nodeTypes.description: Describes the Resources and Locations required to meet an operational scenario from an integrated systems point of view. It is used to communicate overall quantitative and qualitative system characteristics to stakeholders

nodeTypes.label: Information
nodeTypes.description: A comment that describes the state of an item of interest in any medium or form -- and is communicated or received.

nodeTypes.label: InformationElement
nodeTypes.description: An item of information that flows between OperationalPerformers and is produced and consumed by the OperationalActivities that the OperationalPerformers are capable to perform (see IsCapableToPerform).

nodeTypes.label: InformationRole
nodeTypes.description: A usage of InformationElement that exists in the context of an OperationalAsset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of InformationElements.

nodeTypes.label: InteractionMessage
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type that groups several types of messages used in the InteractionScenario.

nodeTypes.label: InteractionRole
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type that represents an individual participant in the InteractionScenario.

nodeTypes.label: KnownResource
nodeTypes.description: Asserts that a known ResourcePerformer constrains the implementation of the OperationalPerformer that plays the role in the LogicalArchitecture.

nodeTypes.label: L1 - Node Types
nodeTypes.description: The L1 Viewpoint defines all of the Nodes that will appear in a Logical Architecture. Nodes are elements of capability assembled and orchestrated in the Logical Architecture (see L2 - Logical Scenario and L4 - Logical Activities views). The levels of capability provided by each node are expressed as Measures of Performance (MoPs) and these may be dependent on the environments in which the Node is required to operate.

nodeTypes.label: L2 - Logical Scenario
nodeTypes.description: The L2 Viewpoint specifies Nodes (elements of capability) in context of each other. The context is usually expressed in terms of the information that flows between the nodes (e.g. the information flow requirements between capabilities in a given scenario) but may also be flows of materiel, human resource or energy.

nodeTypes.label: L2-L3 - Logical Concept
nodeTypes.description: The L2-L3 Viewpoint is concerned with providing an executive level, scenario-based communication of the architecture purpose, scope and content.

nodeTypes.label: L3 - Node Interactions
nodeTypes.description: L3 is used to provide further detail of the interoperability requirements associated with the operational capability of interest. The focus is on logical flows that cross the capability boundary. Although the primary purpose of the L3 Viewpoint is to specify information exchanges, the L3 may also list flows of materiel, energy and human resources.

nodeTypes.label: L4 - Logical Activities
nodeTypes.description: The L4 Viewpoint describes the operational activities that are being conducted within the mission or scenario. These activities are defined at a logical, solution-neutral level so as to enable different solutions in the physical layer. The L4 Viewpoint describes the activities associated with the logical architecture, as well as the:

nodeTypes.label: L4-P4 - Activity To Function Mapping
nodeTypes.description: The L4-P4 Viewpoint depicts the mapping of Resource Functions (and optionally, the resources that provide them) to operational activities and/or service functions. For operational activities it thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution. For service functions it provides the link between the services used at the operational level and the specific Resource Functions provided by the resources supporting the services.

nodeTypes.label: L5 - Logical States
nodeTypes.description: The L5 Viewpoint specifies the typical states a node may have and the possible transitions between those states (i.e. changes of state). Triggers for state changes may also be defined. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g. triggers and events).

nodeTypes.label: L6 - Logical Sequence
nodeTypes.description: Operational Event-Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of interactions between nodes in a scenario or critical sequence of events. The node interactions usually correspond to flows of information but may describe flows of energy, materiel or people specified in the L2, Logical Scenario.

nodeTypes.label: L7 - Information Model
nodeTypes.description: The L7 Viewpoint is used to document the business information. It describes the information that can be exchanged along the logical flows in the architecture, specified in L3 Node Interactions.

nodeTypes.label: L8 - Logical Constraints
nodeTypes.description: The L8 Viewpoint is used to constrain the logical architecture without forcing a particular solution. L8 is used for rules which are not expressed as behavioural models, interactions or measures of effectiveness, i.e. they are textual statements of requirements that constrain the architecture.

nodeTypes.label: Location
nodeTypes.description: A specification of the generic area in which a LocationHolder is required to be located.

nodeTypes.label: LocationHolder
nodeTypes.description: Abstract type, used to group elements that are allowed to be associated with a Location.

nodeTypes.label: Lr - Lines of Development
nodeTypes.description: The Lr Viewpoint is primarily intended to support the acquisition process across multiple projects or programmes, highlighting dependencies the logical dependencies between capabilities, projects and the integration of all lines of development to achieve a successfully integrated military capability. Use of the Lr Viewpoint should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with Cr, Capability Roadmap.

nodeTypes.label: MeasurableElement
nodeTypes.description: Abstract type, grouping elements that can be measured by applying MeasurementSets to them.

nodeTypes.label: Measurement
nodeTypes.description: A property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure.

nodeTypes.label: MeasurementType
nodeTypes.description: A type of a property representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure.

nodeTypes.label: Metadata
nodeTypes.description: A comment that can be applied to any element in the architecture. The attributes associated with this element details the relationship between the element and its related dublinCoreElement, metaDataScheme, category and name. This allows the element to be referenced using the Semantic Web.

nodeTypes.label: NaturalResource
nodeTypes.description: Type of physical resource that occurs in nature.

nodeTypes.label: NonfunctionalRequirement
nodeTypes.description: The element represents a non-functional requirement (how should the system / software be able to do something?).

nodeTypes.label: OperationalActivity
nodeTypes.description: An Activity that captures a logical process, specified independently of how the process is carried out.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalActivityAction
nodeTypes.description: A call of an OperationalActivity in the context of another OperationalActivity.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalActivityEdge
nodeTypes.description: A tuple that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between OperationalActivityActions.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalAgent
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type grouping Operational Architecture and Operational Performer.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalArchitecture
nodeTypes.description: A type used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Operational perspective.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalAsset
nodeTypes.description: An abstract element used to group the elements of OperationalAgent and InformationElement allowing them to own InformationRoles.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalConstraint
nodeTypes.description: A Rule governing a logical architectural element i.e. OperationalPerformer, OperationalActivity, InformationElement etc.

attributes.name: Id
attributes.description: Lesbare laufende Nummerierung f�r den Forderungskatalog

attributes.name: Uuid
attributes.description: Maschinenlesbare Nummerierung f�r das Requirements Management Tool

nodeTypes.label: OperationalExchangeItem
nodeTypes.description: An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between OperationalPerformers and conveyed by an OperationalExchange.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalInterface
nodeTypes.description: A declaration that specifies a contract between the OperationalPerformer it is related to, and any other OperationalPerformers it can interact with.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalMessage
nodeTypes.description: Message for use in an Operational Event-Trace which carries any of the subtypes of OperationalExchange.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalMethod
nodeTypes.description: behavioral feature of a OperationalPerformer whose behavior is specified in an OperationalActivity.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalParameter
nodeTypes.description: A type that represents inputs and outputs of an OperationalActivity. It is typed by an OperationalExchangeItem.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalPerformer
nodeTypes.description: A logical entity that IsCapableToPerform OperationalActivities which produce, consume and process Resources.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalPort
nodeTypes.description: An interaction point for an OperationalAgent through which it can interact with the outside environment and which is defined by an OperationalInterface.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalRole
nodeTypes.description: Usage of a OperationalPerformer or OperationalArchitecture in the context of another OperationalPerformer or OperationalArchitecture. Creates a whole-part relationship.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalSignal
nodeTypes.description: An item of information that flows between OperationalPerformers and is produced and consumed by the OperationalActivities that the OperationalPerformers are capable of performing (see IsCapableToPerform).

nodeTypes.label: OperationalSignalProperty
nodeTypes.description: A property of an OperationalSignal typed by OperationalExchangeItem. It enables OperationalExchangeItem e.g. InformationElement to be passed as arguments of the OperationalSignal.

nodeTypes.label: OperationalStateDescription
nodeTypes.description: A state machine describing the behavior of a OperationalPerformer, depicting how the OperationalPerformer responds to various events and the actions.

nodeTypes.label: Organization
nodeTypes.description: A group of OrganizationalResources (Persons, Posts, Organizations and Responsibilities) associated for a particular purpose.

nodeTypes.label: OrganizationalResource
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type for Organization, Person Post and Responsibility.

nodeTypes.label: P1 - Resource Types
nodeTypes.description: The P1 Viewpoint collects together all the Resource Types in the architecture together with a depiction of their performance characteristics. P1 also provides a summary of the technologies and competencies that impact on the Resources that constitute the architecture.

nodeTypes.label: P2 - Resource Structure
nodeTypes.description: The P2 Viewpoint links together the operational and physical architecture viewpoints by depicting how types of Resource are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in L2, Logical Scenario. The P2 Viewpoint may represent the realization of a requirement specified in a L2 (i.e. in a �to-be� architecture) and so there may be many alternative Resource viewpoint suites that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an �as-is� architecture, a L2 may just be a simplified, logical representation of the P2 Viewpoint to allow communication of key information flows to non-technical stakeholders.

nodeTypes.label: P3 - Resource Connectivity
nodeTypes.description: The networks and pathways documented in a P3 Viewpoint represent the physical implementation of the logical flows identified in a L2, Logical Scenario, or L3, Node Interactions View.

nodeTypes.label: P4 - Resource Functions
nodeTypes.description: The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the allocation of functions to resources, and flows between Resource Functions. The P4 Viewpoint is the Physical Resource counterpart to the L4 Logical Activities Viewpoint.

nodeTypes.label: P5 - Resource States
nodeTypes.description: The P5 Viewpoint identifies the states a Resource can be, the allowable changes between those states, and the stimuli (e.g. triggers and events) that cause the state changes.

nodeTypes.label: P6 - Resource Sequence
nodeTypes.description: The P6 Viewpoint is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and Resource Interactions, and to ensure that each participating Resource or Port has the necessary information, at the right time, in order to perform its assigned functionality.

nodeTypes.label: P7 - Data Model
nodeTypes.description: The P7 Viewpoint is one of the architectural products closest to actual system design in the NAF. It is used to describe how the information represented in the L7 Logical Data Model Viewpoint is implemented.

nodeTypes.label: P8 - Resource Constraints
nodeTypes.description: The P8 Viewpoint describes constraints on the Resources, Resource Functions, data and communications that make up a Physical Architecture.

nodeTypes.label: PaperForm
nodeTypes.description: Form is a digitized or digitizable document, for example a scanned document.

nodeTypes.label: Person
nodeTypes.description: A type of a human being used to define the characteristics that need to be described for ActualPersons (e.g. properties such as address, telephone number, nationality, etc).

nodeTypes.label: PhysicalResource
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type defining physical resources (i.e. OrganizationalResource, ResourceArtifact and NaturalResource).

nodeTypes.label: Post
nodeTypes.description: A type of job title or position that a person can fill (e.g. Lawyer, Solution Architect, Machine Operator or Chief Executive Officer).

nodeTypes.label: PostRole
nodeTypes.description: A usage of a post in the context of another OrganizationalResource. Creates a whole-part relationship.

nodeTypes.label: Pr - Configuration Management
nodeTypes.description: The Pr Viewpoint provides an overview of how Resource Assets change over time (note that NAF v3.1 only allowed for Capability Configurations whereas now this is opened up to all Resource Types). It shows the structure of different versions of Resource Assets (usually Capability Configurations or Service Implementations) mapped against a timeline.

nodeTypes.label: ProblemDomain
nodeTypes.description: A property associated with a logical architecture, used to specify the scope of the problem.

nodeTypes.label: ProcessEdge
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type that represents a behavior or process (i.e. a Function or OperationalActivity) that can be performed by a Performer.

nodeTypes.label: ProcessMessageFlow
nodeTypes.description: A tuple that shows the flow of message between different ActivityPartitions like Pools.

nodeTypes.label: ProcessOperation
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type that represents a behavior or process (i.e. a Function or OperationalActivity) that can be performed by a Performer.

nodeTypes.label: ProcessParameter
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type that represents a behavior or process (i.e. a Function or OperationalActivity) that can be performed by a Performer.

nodeTypes.label: ProcessUsage
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type that represents a behavior or process (i.e. a Function or OperationalActivity) that can be performed by a Performer or Role.

nodeTypes.label: Project
nodeTypes.description: A type that describes types of time-limited endeavours that are required to meet one or more Capability needs.

attributes.id: Architectural guidelines of the Federal Government
attributes.name: Architectural guidelines of the Federal Government

attributes.id: Service docum. according to IT-Service Designer Bw
attributes.name: Service docum. according to IT-Service Designer Bw

attributes.id: Used templates and specifications for SIR
attributes.name: Used templates and specifications for SIR

attributes.id: Used templates and specifications for SSO
attributes.name: Used templates and specifications for SSO

nodeTypes.label: ProjectMilestone
nodeTypes.description: A type of event in a Project by which progress is measured.

nodeTypes.label: ProjectMilestoneRole
nodeTypes.description: The role played by a ProjectMilestone in the context of a Project.

nodeTypes.label: ProjectRole
nodeTypes.description: Usage of a Project in the context of another Project. Creates a whole-part relationship.

nodeTypes.label: ProjectStatus
nodeTypes.description: The status (i.e. level of progress) of a ProjectTheme for an ActualProject at the time of the ActualProjectMilestone.

nodeTypes.label: ProjectTheme
nodeTypes.description: A property of a ProjectMilestone that captures an aspect by which the progress of ActualProjects may be measured.

nodeTypes.label: PropertySet
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type grouping architectural elements that can own Measurements.

nodeTypes.label: Protocol
nodeTypes.description: A Standard for communication over a network. Protocols may be composite, represented as a ProtocolStack made up of ProtocolLayers.

nodeTypes.label: ProtocolImplementation
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type grouping architectural elements that can implement Protocols.

nodeTypes.label: ProtocolLayer
nodeTypes.description: Usage of a Protocol in the context of another Protocol. Creates a whole-part relationship.

nodeTypes.label: Protocolstack
nodeTypes.description: A sub type of Protocol that contains the ProtocolLayers, defining a complete stack.

nodeTypes.label: ProvidedServiceLevel
nodeTypes.description: A sub type of ActualService that details a specific service level delivered by the provider.

nodeTypes.label: R2 - Requirement Catalogue
nodeTypes.description: The R2 represents a catalog of requirements in the architectural model. For this purpose, categories can be created in which the functional and non-functional requirements can be grouped.

nodeTypes.label: R3 - Requirement Dependencies
nodeTypes.description: The R3 is used to show dependencies between different requirements.

nodeTypes.label: R7 - Requirement Derivation
nodeTypes.description: The R7 assigns functional and non-functional requirements to the demanding architectural elements. In addition, information about the planned realization can be shown.

nodeTypes.label: R8 - Requirement Fulfilment
nodeTypes.description: The R8 is used to determine and map acceptance and evaluation criteria for the individual functional and non-functional requirements.

nodeTypes.label: Recommendation
nodeTypes.description: Need for action from a finding.

nodeTypes.label: Reference
nodeTypes.description: Element describes all types of references.

nodeTypes.label: RequiredServiceLevel
nodeTypes.description: A sub type of ActualService that details a specific service level required of the provider.

nodeTypes.label: RequirementCatalogue
nodeTypes.description: Element represents a catalog of requirements, which consists of different categories (RequirementCategory) of functional and non-functional requirements.

nodeTypes.label: RequirementCategory
nodeTypes.description: Element represents a category of a catalog of requirements.

nodeTypes.label: Resource
nodeTypes.description: Abstract element grouping for all elements that can be conveyed by an Exchange.

nodeTypes.label: ResourceArchitecture
nodeTypes.description: A type used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the ResourcePerformer perspective.

nodeTypes.label: ResourceArtifact
nodeTypes.description: A type of man-made object that contains no human beings (i.e. satellite, radio, petrol, gasoline, etc.).

nodeTypes.label: ResourceAsset
nodeTypes.description: An abstract element used to group the elements of ResourcePerformer and DataElement allowing them to own DataRoles

nodeTypes.label: ResourceConstraint
nodeTypes.description: A rule governing the structural or functional aspects of an implementation.

nodeTypes.label: ResourceExchangeItem
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type grouping elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between ResourcePerformers and conveyed by a ResourceExchange.

nodeTypes.label: ResourceInterface
nodeTypes.description: A declaration that specifies a contract between the ResourcePerformers it is related to and any other ResourcePerformers it can interact with. It is also intended to be an implementation of a specification of an Interface in the Business and/or Service layer.

nodeTypes.label: ResourceMessage
nodeTypes.description: Message for use in an Resource Event-Trace which carries any of the subtypes of ResourceExchange.

nodeTypes.label: ResourceMethod
nodeTypes.description: A behavioral feature of a ResourcePerformer whose behavior is specified in a Function.

nodeTypes.label: ResourceMitigation
nodeTypes.description: A set of measures intended to implement an OperationalMitigation. Comprises a subset of activities that are performed in mitigation of the risk to protect the asset that is the subject of risk (ResourceRole) at the physical level. In the case of a Risk applicable to security, the form of activity is a SecurityControl or an EnhancedSecurityControl, otherwise it is a Function.

nodeTypes.label: ResourceParameter
nodeTypes.description: A type that represents inputs and outputs of an Function. It is typed by a ResourceInteractionItem.

nodeTypes.label: ResourcePerformer
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type grouping elements that can be the subject of a SecurityConstraint (there are currently no security viewpoints in the NAF).

nodeTypes.label: ResourcePort
nodeTypes.description: An interaction point for a ResourcePerformer through which it can interact with the outside environment and which is defined by a ResourceInterface.

nodeTypes.label: ResourceRole
nodeTypes.description: Usage of a ResourcePerformer in the context of another ResourcePerformer. Creates a whole-part relationship.

nodeTypes.label: ResourceSignal
nodeTypes.description: A property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure.

nodeTypes.label: ResourceSignalProperty
nodeTypes.description: A property of an ResourceSignal typed by ResourceExchangeItem. It enables ResourceExchangeItem e.g. DataElement to be passed as arguments of the ResourceSignal.

nodeTypes.label: ResourceStateDescription
nodeTypes.description: A state machine describing the behavior of a ResourcePerformer, depicting how the ResourcePerformer responds to various events and the actions.

nodeTypes.label: Responsibility
nodeTypes.description: The type of duty required of a Person or Organization.

nodeTypes.label: Rr - Requirement Realization
nodeTypes.description: In the Rr, requirements are assigned to the realizing architectural elements.

nodeTypes.label: Rule
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type for all types of constraint (i.e. an OperationalConstraint could detail the rules of accountancy best practice).

nodeTypes.label: S1 - Service Taxonomy
nodeTypes.description: The purpose of the S1 Viewpoint is to provide a governance structure for a Service-Oriented Architecture. Along with S3, Service Interfaces, it specifies a standard library of service specifications for an enterprise, to which service implementers are expected to conform.

nodeTypes.label: S2 - Service Structure
nodeTypes.description: The S2 Viewpoint is concerned with identification and description of how services are structured to create a higher-aggregated service. To provide highlevel views, dependencies to other services, nodes and resources as well as service interfaces and service functions can be represented.

nodeTypes.label: S3 - Service Interfaces
nodeTypes.description: The S3 Viewpoint is concerned with the identification and definition of service interfaces.

nodeTypes.label: S4 - Service Functions
nodeTypes.description: The S4 Viewpoint is the key behavioural specification for services. Equivalent in nature to L4, Logical Activities and P4, Resources Functions, it specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform. Implementation of that behaviour is represented in P4, Resource Functions, and L4-P4, Activity to Function Mapping.

nodeTypes.label: S5 - Service States
nodeTypes.description:
The S5 Viewpoint is a specification of the allowable states of a service, and the possible transitions between them. This specification constrains how implementations of the service will behave.

It is, though, generally considered a good practice to make services stateless - i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in.

nodeTypes.label: S6 - Service Interactions
nodeTypes.description: Service Interaction Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of interactions between services in a composition or critical sequence of events.

nodeTypes.label: S7 - Service Interface Parameters
nodeTypes.description: Specifies the interfaces that a service provides and uses, defines which services are compatible with which other services.

nodeTypes.label: S8 - Service Policy
nodeTypes.description: The S8 Viewpoint specifies constraints against services to which implementations must conform.

nodeTypes.label: SecurityConstraint
nodeTypes.description: A type of rule that captures a formal statement to define security laws, regulations, guidances, and policy. Element is not used in the current version of the framework and reserved for future developments.

nodeTypes.label: SecurityEnclave
nodeTypes.description: Collection of information systems connected by one or more internal networks under the control of a single authority and security policy. The systems may be structured by physical proximity or by function, independent of location.  Element is not used in the current version of the framework and reserved for future developments.

nodeTypes.label: SecurityProcess
nodeTypes.description: The security-related procedure that satisfies the security control requirement. Element is not used in the current version of the framework and reserved for future developments.

nodeTypes.label: ServiceFunction
nodeTypes.description: An Activity that describes the abstract behavior of ServiceSpecifications, regardless of the actual implementation.

nodeTypes.label: ServiceFunctionAction
nodeTypes.description: A call of a ServiceFunction in the context of another ServiceFunction.

nodeTypes.label: ServiceInterface
nodeTypes.description: A contract that defines the ServiceMethods and ServiceMessageHandlers that the ServiceSpecification realizes.

nodeTypes.label: ServiceMessage
nodeTypes.description: Message for use in a Service Event-Trace.

nodeTypes.label: ServiceMethod
nodeTypes.description: A behavioral feature of a ServiceSpecification whose behavior is specified in a ServiceFunction.

nodeTypes.label: ServiceParameter
nodeTypes.description: A type that represents inputs and outputs of a ServiceFunction, represents inputs and outputs of a ServiceSpecification.

nodeTypes.label: ServicePolicy
nodeTypes.description: A constraint governing the use of one or more ServiceSpecifications.

nodeTypes.label: ServicePort
nodeTypes.description: An interaction point for a ServiceSpecification through which it can interact with the outside environment and which is defined by a ServiceInterface.

nodeTypes.label: ServiceSpecification
nodeTypes.description: The specification of a set of functionality provided one element for the use of others.

nodeTypes.label: ServiceSpecificationRole
nodeTypes.description: An assertion that a ServiceSecification calls upon another ServiceSpecification in order to deliver its stated functionality.

nodeTypes.label: ServiceStateDescription
nodeTypes.description: A state machine descripting the behavior of a ServiceSpecification, depicting how the ServiceSpecification responds to various events and the actions.

nodeTypes.label: SMEReference
nodeTypes.description: Element stands for a result of a workshop or expert knowledge.

nodeTypes.label: Software
nodeTypes.description: A sub-type of ResourceArtifact that specifies an executable computer program.

nodeTypes.label: Sr - Service Roadmap
nodeTypes.description: The Sr Viewpoint supports the Service Audit process by providing a method to identify gaps or duplications in service provision. Sr indicates service lifetime evolutions, which are derived from delivery milestones within acquisition projects.

nodeTypes.label: Stakeholder
nodeTypes.description: individual, team, organization, or classes thereof, having an interest in an EnterprisePhase [ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2011].

nodeTypes.label: Standard
nodeTypes.description: A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture.

nodeTypes.label: StandardOperationalActivity
nodeTypes.description: A sub-type of OperationalActivity that is a standard operating procedure.

nodeTypes.label: StateDescription
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type that represents a state machine (i.e. an OperationalStateDescription or ResourceStateDescription), depicting how the Asset responds to various events and the actions.

nodeTypes.label: StrategicConstraint
nodeTypes.description: A Rule governing a capability.

nodeTypes.label: SubjectOfForecast
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type grouping elements that can be the subject of a Forecast.

nodeTypes.label: SubjectOfOperationalConstraint
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type grouping elements that can be the subject of an OperationalConstraint.

nodeTypes.label: SubjectOfResourceConstraint
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type grouping elements that can be the subject of a ResourceConstraint.

nodeTypes.label: SubjectOfSecurityConstraint
nodeTypes.description: An abstract grouping of elements that can be the subject of a SecurityConstraint. Element is not used in the current version of the framework and reserved for future developments.

nodeTypes.label: SubOrganization
nodeTypes.description: A type of a human being used to define the characteristics that need to be described for ActualPersons (e.g. properties such as address, telephone number, nationality, etc).

nodeTypes.label: System
nodeTypes.description: An integrated set of elements, subsystems, or assemblies that accomplish a defined objective. These elements include products (hardware, software, firmware), processes, people, information, techniques, facilities, services, and other support elements (INCOSE SE Handbook V4, 2015).

nodeTypes.label: Technology
nodeTypes.description: A sub type of ResourceArtifact that indicates a technology domain, i.e. nuclear, mechanical, electronic, mobile telephony etc.

nodeTypes.label: TemporalPart
nodeTypes.description: A current or future state of the wholeLifeEnterprise or another EnterprisePhase.

nodeTypes.label: UAFElement
nodeTypes.description: Abstract super type for all of the UAF elements. It provides a way for all of the UAF elements to have a common set of properties.

nodeTypes.label: VersionedElement
nodeTypes.description: An abstract type grouping ResourcePerformer and ServiceSpecification that allows VersionOfConfiguration to be related to ActualProjectMilestones.

nodeTypes.label: VersionOfConfiguration
nodeTypes.description: A property of a WholeLifeConfiguration, used in version control of a VersionedElement. It asserts that a VersionedElement is a version of a WholeLifeConfiguration.

nodeTypes.label: View
nodeTypes.description: An architecture view expresses the architecture of the system-of-interest in accordance with an architecture viewpoint (or simply, viewpoint). [ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2011(E)].

nodeTypes.label: Viewpoint
nodeTypes.description: An architecture viewpoint frames (to formulate or construct in a particular style or language) one or more concerns. A concern can be framed by more than one viewpoint.  [ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2011(E)].

nodeTypes.label: WholeLifeConfiguration
nodeTypes.description: A set of VersionedElements.

nodeTypes.label: WholeLifeEnterprise
nodeTypes.description: A WholeLifeEnterprise is a purposeful endeavor of any size involving people, organizations and supporting systems. It is made up of TemporalParts and StructuralParts.

edgeTypes.label: AchievedEffect
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that exists between an ActualState  (e.g., observed/measured during testing) of an element that attempts to achieve a DesiredEffect and an Achiever.

edgeTypes.label: ActivityPerformableUnderCondition
edgeTypes.description: The ActualCondition under which an Activity is performed.

edgeTypes.label: ActivitySupportsService
edgeTypes.description: Relation states that a process is necessary for the implementation of a service.

edgeTypes.label: ActsUpon
edgeTypes.description: Asserts that something (subject) is acted upon by an OperationalActivity (activity).

edgeTypes.label: ActualConditionToActualResource
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that a actual resource is an actual situation.

edgeTypes.label: ActualMeasurementSetAppliesFor
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which actual measurement applies for an element.

edgeTypes.label: ActualProjectConsults
edgeTypes.description: A relation that expresses that a project consults an OrganizationalResource.

edgeTypes.label: ActualProjectDependency
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that is a dependency of a actualproject on a actualproject.

edgeTypes.label: ActualProjectInforms
edgeTypes.description: A relation that expresses that a project informs an OrganizationalResource.

edgeTypes.label: ActualResourceNeededByActualProjectMilestone
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that an actual resource is needed by actual project milestones.

edgeTypes.label: ActualResourceRelationship
edgeTypes.description: An actual resource flow existing between ActualResources (i.e. flow of data, people, materiel, or energy).

edgeTypes.label: ActualResourceToActualProjectMilestone
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that an actual resource is mapped to actual project milestones.

edgeTypes.label: AffectedActivity
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which resource is affected by a operational activity.

edgeTypes.label: AffectedFunctions
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which function is affected by a resource.

edgeTypes.label: AffectedResource
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which operational activity is affected by a resource.

edgeTypes.label: ArbitraryConnector
edgeTypes.description: Represents a visual indication of a connection used in high level operational concept diagrams.

edgeTypes.label: ArchitecturalReference
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that specifies that one architectural description refers to another.

edgeTypes.label: ArchitecturalSequence
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that specifies that one architectural description is the successor of another.

edgeTypes.label: ArchitectureForProject
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that a architectural description belongs to a actual project.

edgeTypes.label: BoundaryCondition
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which environment is relevant to an resource exchange.

edgeTypes.label: CapabilityDependency
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that asserts that one Capability is dependent from another.

edgeTypes.label: CapabilityForTask
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that asserts that a Capability is required in order for an Enterprise to conduct a phase of an EnduringTask.

edgeTypes.label: CapabilityGeneralization
edgeTypes.description: A CapabilityGeneralization is a taxonomic relationship between a more general Capability and a more specific Capability.

edgeTypes.label: CapabilityRoleDependency
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that asserts that one CapabilityRole is dependent from another.

edgeTypes.label: Checks
edgeTypes.description: Relation that shows that an acceptance criterion (FitCriterion) is valid for a functional or non-functional requirement.

edgeTypes.label: Classified
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that indicates which classification an element has.

edgeTypes.label: Command
edgeTypes.description: A type of ResourceExchange that asserts that one OrganizationalResource commands another.

edgeTypes.label: CompetenceForRole
edgeTypes.description: A tuple used to associate an organizational role with a specific set of required competencies.

edgeTypes.label: CompliesViewpoint
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that expresses that a view has been created according to the specifications of a viewpoint.

edgeTypes.label: ConcernForActualEnterprisePhase
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which concerns are covered by an actual enterprise phase.

edgeTypes.label: ConcernForView
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which concerns are covered by view.

edgeTypes.label: ConcernForViewpoint
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which concerns are covered by viewpoint.

edgeTypes.label: ConflictsWith
edgeTypes.description: Relation that represents a conflict between two requirements.

edgeTypes.label: ConformsTo
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that an UAFElement conforms to a standard.

edgeTypes.label: ConsumedBy
edgeTypes.description: Asserts that a service is consumed by a node. It is not required to know what provides the service.

edgeTypes.label: Consumes
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that asserts that a service in someway contributes or assists in the execution of an OperationalActivity.

edgeTypes.label: Control
edgeTypes.description: A type of ResourceExchange that asserts that one PhysicalResource controls another PhysicalResource (i.e.  the driver of a vehicle controlling the vehicle speed or direction).

edgeTypes.label: DataElementStoredIn
edgeTypes.description: Relation says that a data is stored in software.

edgeTypes.label: DerivedFrom
edgeTypes.description: Relation that shows that a functional or non-functional requirement is based on a process, role and task carrier, information element or other element.

edgeTypes.label: DescribedBy
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that an architectural description describes an architecture.

edgeTypes.label: DesiredEffect
edgeTypes.description: A tuple relating the Desirer (a Capability or OrganizationalResource) to an ActualState.

edgeTypes.label: EnvironmentalCondition
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that indicates under which environment an exhibits-relationship takes place.

edgeTypes.label: EnvironmentalContext
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that indicates under which condition an measurement counts.

edgeTypes.label: Evaluates
edgeTypes.description: This relation states that an evaluation criterion (FulfilmentCriterion) can be assigned to a specific acceptance criterion (FitCriterion).

edgeTypes.label: Exhibits
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that exists between a CapableElement and a Capability that it meets under specific environmental conditions.

edgeTypes.label: Expresses
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that an architectural description includes the following architectures.

edgeTypes.label: FillsPost
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that asserts that an ActualPerson fills an ActualPost.

edgeTypes.label: Forecast
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that specifies a transition from one Asset, Standard, Competence to another future one. It is related to an ActualEnterprisePhase to give it a temporal context.

edgeTypes.label: ForecastPeriod
edgeTypes.description: Planning phase for which the forecast is valid.

edgeTypes.label: FormStoredIn
edgeTypes.description: Relation states that a digital form is stored in software.

edgeTypes.label: FunctionControlFlow
edgeTypes.description: An ActivityEdge that shows the flow of control between FunctionActions.

edgeTypes.label: FunctionObjectFlow
edgeTypes.description: An ActivityEdge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/data) between FunctionActions.

edgeTypes.label: FunctionSubject
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that a function uses certain resources.

edgeTypes.label: GoalForActualEnterprisePhase
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which actual enterprisephase implements an enterprisegoal.

edgeTypes.label: HostedOn
edgeTypes.description: Relation states that hardware (virtualized) or software is hosted on a virtualized platform or physical hardware.

edgeTypes.label: Implements
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that defines how an element in the upper layer of abstraction is implemented by a semantically equivalent element (i.e. tracing the OperationalActivities to the Functions that implement them) in the lower level of abstraction.

edgeTypes.label: ImplementsProtocol
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which protocol implements an architectural element.

edgeTypes.label: IsAccountableFor
edgeTypes.description: A relation that expresses that an OrganizationalResource is responsible for a resource, service or project in the context of an approval.

edgeTypes.label: IsCapableToPerform
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that says that a capable element performs an activity or action.

edgeTypes.label: IsDuplicateOf
edgeTypes.description: Relation that represents that two requirements convey the same content.

edgeTypes.label: IsEquivalentToStandardElement
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that indicates that a model element corresponds to a catalog element.

edgeTypes.label: IsResponsibleFor
edgeTypes.description: A relation that expresses that an OrganizationalResource is responsible for a resource, service or project.

edgeTypes.label: JustifiedBy
edgeTypes.description: Relation states that an Constraint is derived from a reference (Reference, DocumentReference, SMEReference).

edgeTypes.label: LocationType
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which location is assigned to a location holder.

edgeTypes.label: MapsToCapability
edgeTypes.description: A tuple denoting that an Activity contributes to providing a Capability.

edgeTypes.label: MilestoneDependency
edgeTypes.description: A tuple between two ActualProjectMilestones that denotes one ActualProjectMilestone follows from another.

edgeTypes.label: NeedsModificationOf
edgeTypes.description: Relation stats that a project makes adjustments to a resource.

edgeTypes.label: NeedsResource
edgeTypes.description: Relation stats that a project needs a resource.

edgeTypes.label: NeedsService
edgeTypes.description: A relation that expresses that a project needs a service

edgeTypes.label: OperationalArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that says that in a actual enterprisephase an operational architecture is valid.

edgeTypes.label: OperationalConnector
edgeTypes.description: A Connector that goes between OperationalRoles representing a need to exchange Resources. It can carry a number of OperationalExchanges.

edgeTypes.label: OperationalControlFlow
edgeTypes.description: An ActivityEdge that shows the flow of control between OperationalActivityActions.

edgeTypes.label: OperationalExchange
edgeTypes.description: Asserts that a flow can exist between OperationalPerformers (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).

edgeTypes.label: OperationalMessageFlow
edgeTypes.description: A ProcessMessageFlow that shows the flow of message between OperationalActivityActions of different ActivityPartitions like Pools.

edgeTypes.label: OperationalObjectFlow
edgeTypes.description: An ActivityEdge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between OperationalActivityActions.

edgeTypes.label: OriginatesFrom
edgeTypes.description: Relation that derives an element in the architectural model from a reference (Reference, DocumentReference, SMEReference).

edgeTypes.label: OwnedMilestone
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that expresses that actual project has a actual milestone.

edgeTypes.label: OwnsActualMeasurementSet
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which actual measurement set an element owns.

edgeTypes.label: OwnsMeasurement
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which measurement or measurement type an element owns.

edgeTypes.label: OwnsProcess
edgeTypes.description: A dependency relationship denoting that an ActualOrganizationResource owns an OperationalActivity.

edgeTypes.label: PartOfCatalogue
edgeTypes.description: This relation states that a category (RequirementCategory) belongs to a requirements catalog (RequirementCatalogue).

edgeTypes.label: PartOfCategory
edgeTypes.description: This relation states that his functional or non-functional requirement belongs to a category (RequirementCategory) of the requirements catalog.

edgeTypes.label: PerformsInContext
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that says that a role performs an activity or action. It indicates that the action can be carried out by the role when used in a specific context or configuration.

edgeTypes.label: PhysicalArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that an actual enterprise phase has resource architectures.

edgeTypes.label: PhysicalLocation
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that a location holder operates in an actual location.

edgeTypes.label: ProcessGeneralization
edgeTypes.description: A ProcessGeneralization is a taxonomic relationship between a more general Process and a more specific Process.

edgeTypes.label: ProjectMilestoneToProjectTheme
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which project theme is handled by which project milestone.

edgeTypes.label: ProjectProvidesFunction
edgeTypes.description: Relation stats that a project realizes a function.

edgeTypes.label: ProjectSequence
edgeTypes.description: A tuple between two ActualProjects that denotes one ActualProject cannot start before the previous ActualProject is finished.

edgeTypes.label: ProjectSupportActivity
edgeTypes.description: Relation stats that a project supports an activity.

edgeTypes.label: PropertySetGeneralization
edgeTypes.description: A PropertySetGeneralization is a taxonomic relationship between a more general PropertySet and a more specific PropertySet.

edgeTypes.label: Provides
edgeTypes.description: Asserts that a operational agent provides a service.

edgeTypes.label: ProvidesCompetence
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that asserts that an ActualOrganizationalResource provides a specific set of Competencies.

edgeTypes.label: ProvidesServiceFunction
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that expresses that a service function is provided by an interface.

edgeTypes.label: RatifiedStandards
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that an actual organization releases a standard.

edgeTypes.label: RealiseRequirement
edgeTypes.description: Relation states that a functional or non-functional requirement is realized through this element.

edgeTypes.label: RealizedDesiredEffect
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that expresses which connector DesiredEffect the connector AchievedEffect realizes.

edgeTypes.label: RealizesRecommendation
edgeTypes.description: Relation states that a Recommendation is realized through this element.

edgeTypes.label: RealizingAchievedEffect
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that expresses which connector AchievedEffect realizes the connector DesiredEffect.

edgeTypes.label: RefersTo
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that assigns a finding to a recommendation.

edgeTypes.label: Refines
edgeTypes.description: Relation that represents a refinement of a requirement by another requirement.

edgeTypes.label: Replaces
edgeTypes.description: Relation that represents a replacement of a requirement with another requirement.

edgeTypes.label: ReplaceStandardElement
edgeTypes.description: Relation that represents a replacement of a standard element with another standard element

edgeTypes.label: RequiredEnvironment
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that a location holder operates under specific environmental conditions.

edgeTypes.label: RequiredResource
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that indicates which resources a project milestone requires

edgeTypes.label: Requires
edgeTypes.description: Relation that represents that a requirement assumes another requirement.

edgeTypes.label: RequiresCompetence
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that asserts that an ActualOrganizationalResource is required to have a specific set of Competencies.

edgeTypes.label: ResourceConnector
edgeTypes.description: A channel for exchange between two ResourceRoles.

edgeTypes.label: ResourceDependency
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that is a dependency of a resource on a resource.

edgeTypes.label: ResourceExchange
edgeTypes.description: Asserts that a flow can exist between ResourcePerformers (i.e. flows of data, people, material, or energy).

edgeTypes.label: ResourceToServiceDependency
edgeTypes.description: Relation states that a resource is dependent on a service.

edgeTypes.label: Responsible
edgeTypes.description: Relation states that a project is responsible for a service or a material resource.

edgeTypes.label: ResultsFrom
edgeTypes.description: Relationship expresses that an element of architecture is the reason for a finding.

edgeTypes.label: SameAs
edgeTypes.description: A tuple that asserts that two elements refer to the same real-world thing.

edgeTypes.label: Satisfy
edgeTypes.description: This relation states that an constraint affects an element.

edgeTypes.label: ServiceClassification
edgeTypes.description: Relation is used to show that two services have a relationship in the sense of a taxonomy.

edgeTypes.label: ServiceConnector
edgeTypes.description: A channel for exchange between two ServiceSpecifications. Where one acts as the consumer of the other.

edgeTypes.label: ServiceDependency
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that is a dependency of a service on a service, operational node or resource.

edgeTypes.label: ServiceProvision
edgeTypes.description: An assertion that a Resource delivers a Service to a specified ServiceLevel.

edgeTypes.label: ServiceSpecificationGeneralization
edgeTypes.description: A ServiceSpecificationGeneralization is a taxonomic relationship between a more general ServiceSpecification and a more specific ServiceSpecification.

edgeTypes.label: StakeholderConcern
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which concern a stakeholder has.

edgeTypes.label: StatementTask
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that an actual enterprise phase fulfills a actual enduring task.

edgeTypes.label: StemsFrom
edgeTypes.description: Relationship that states that one requirement stems from another.

edgeTypes.label: StoredIn
edgeTypes.description: Relation states that a digital form or data is stored in software.

edgeTypes.label: SuccessorOf
edgeTypes.description: A relationship between two elements that indicates that one element is the successor of the other.

edgeTypes.label: ToBeRealizedBy
edgeTypes.description: Relation states that a functional or non-functional requirement should be realized through this element.

edgeTypes.label: VersionReleased
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that an actual project milestone releases an versioned element.

edgeTypes.label: VersionSuccession
edgeTypes.description: A tuple between two  VersionOfConfigurations that denotes that one VersionOfConfiguration follows from another.

edgeTypes.label: VersionWithdrawn
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that an actual project milestone withdraws an versioned element.

edgeTypes.label: ViewpointsInArchitecturalDescription
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that an architectural description includes the following viewpoints.

edgeTypes.label: ViewpointToStakeholder
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which stakeholder needs viewpoint.

edgeTypes.label: ViewsInArchitecturalDescription
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses that an architectural description includes the following views.

edgeTypes.label: VisionForActualEnterprisePhase
edgeTypes.description: A relationship that expresses which actual enterprisephase implements an enterprisevision.
